behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git ancestry/commit-age evaluation plus PR-presence lookup, which is typically exercised via git subprocess and repository state. MOCK-MISS: this is not a single pure function; it depends on handoff between git-state collection and stale-rule evaluation. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: faking commit graphs can miss real rev-list/merge-base edge behavior and command semantics. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision (disqualifies Agentic) and no multi-agentic sequence (disqualifies Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo with main + feature branch containing older unmerged commits, ensure no open PR for that branch in the PR source used by the app, run staleness detection end-to-end, and assert it is classified stale.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is a module-boundary orchestration check (session bootstrap wiring + warning renderer + detector result). MOCK-MISS applies at local handoff/order boundary, not OS/network. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real subprocess/network in the behavior text; no AI/ML output dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher levels: text says session-start warning emission, not live external infra requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke session bootstrap with a deterministic stale-branch state fixture from the detector interface, and assert warning emission includes branch name/age/actionable PR guidance in the startup output channel.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets one local deterministic mapping function. MOCK-MISS: no inter-module failure is described; this is pure decision logic. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-step pipeline. Rejected higher levels: disqualified by behavior text 'local status-classifier function' and 'deterministically'.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test of classifier inputs (clean state, stale-without-PR state, boundary age/commit-count variants) asserting exact label outputs and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live repo + live PR state and rendered command output. REAL-INFRA is mandatory: real git/process and real PR source contract can fail in ways mocks hide (field mapping, latency/error surfaces, command/environment behavior). Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML model decision in text (disqualifies Agentic), and no multi-agentic chain (disqualifies Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real repository context with real PR-state backend data for two scenarios (clean branch, unPR'ed stale branch) and assert rendered status text matches expected clean vs STALE classifications.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is often at boundary between local branch evaluation and real PR-state semantics (open vs merged flags, head/base linkage). MOCK-HIDE: stubs frequently oversimplify PR lifecycle fields and can miss real contract mismatches causing false positives. Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic state classification, with no AI/ML output quality or multi-agentic sequencing in text.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise staleness classification end-to-end with real repo branches tied to (a) open PR and (b) merged PR records from the actual PR source integration, then assert both are not labeled `stale-no-pr`.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears with real git history rewriting/topology changes; commit-hash churn after rebase/squash is an OS+git-subprocess/repo-state behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked commit lists can miss merge-base/ancestry nuances after history rewrite. Rejected higher levels: text is about git state correctness, not AI/ML decisions (disqualifies Agentic) and not multi-agentic pipelines (disqualifies Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, create stale candidate branch, then perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios that rewrite hashes while integrating work; rerun detector and assert branch is no longer classified `stale-no-pr`.
